title: Prompt: Setting
description: Generates an evocative place and time to ground a story.
entries: a night market that only appears in the rain
the last library on a drowning coast
a hotel where every room is a different year
a train that never stops, staffed by people who cannot remember boarding
a village at the bottom of a valley that fills with fog at noon
the back office of a lost-and-found that keeps things nobody claims
a lighthouse on a shore the sea has quietly stopped reaching
an orchard where the trees only bear fruit for the grieving
a bathhouse carved into the roots of a mountain
the waiting room of a station between two places that no longer exist
a border town where the two clocks never agree
an abandoned amusement park that lights itself at midnight
a bookshop with a floor that changes each time the door closes
a snowbound observatory watching a sky that has gone dark
a floating city built on the wrecks of the ships that sought it
a garden maze planted to hide something at its center
a diner at the last exit before a very long stretch of nothing
the flooded ground floor of a house the family refuses to leave
a monastery where the monks have taken a vow against a single word
a desert crossing marked only by the bones of what came before
a tenement where every tenant is waiting for the same delivery
an island that appears on no chart younger than a hundred years
a theater that keeps performing a play no one wrote
the quiet car of a ferry crossing a lake with no far shore
a mountain pass where travelers leave letters they can never send
a greenhouse kept warm through a winter that will not end
a coastal town the tide takes back a little more of each year
an all-night laundromat that fills with strangers at the same hour
a ballroom sealed since the night the music stopped
a crossroads inn where the roads out change with the season
a customs house on a river that separates nothing from nothing
a rooftop garden above a city that has forgotten it is there
a chapel at the edge of a quarry that swallows every echo
a boarding school kept open for a single remaining student
a marsh where the paths are only safe at the turning of the tide
a radio station broadcasting from a valley the maps left blank
a cannery long closed, still smelling of the sea it no longer touches
a winter fair on a frozen river that groans beneath the crowd
a switchboard office where the last operator still answers every call
a mountain village reachable only when the pass agrees to open
